reason: The omission is limited to motivational context (default toString behavior and security use case) in the intro sentence. The section's primary purpose — providing the complete customization implementation — is fully covered. Users who reach this section are already seeking customization guidance, so the missing "when/why" context has minimal practical effect. The default behavior of $data$ may also be inferable from adjacent sections (the output items table warning). No functional customization step is missing.

findings_assessment.justification: The missing sentence ('デフォルトでtoStringメソッドにより全てのデータ項目が出力される') and security use-case motivation are context/rationale, not procedural content. The full customization implementation (DataItem extension, getLogItems override, properties config, output example) is present and correct. An AI serving questions about how to customize $data$ can answer fully from this section. Questions about default behavior ('What does $data$ output by default?') are edge cases that may be addressed in sibling sections covering output items. The omission does not cause incorrect guidance. Why it persisted: Phase E produced a knowledge file byte-for-byte identical to Phase D — the fix agent ran but applied no edit to the intro paragraph, likely treating this minor contextual omission as below the threshold for modification.
